exemplar.title: Beauty In Balance : A Guidebook On Skincare

detailedExemplarData.title: Beauty In Balance : A Guidebook On Skincare

strengths: Detailed examples of applying creative, critical thinking and organizational skills during the Canva design process
Effective use of time-management, information-literacy and communication skills, supported by concrete examples
weaknesses: Limited reflection on how challenges during the Canva process developed problem-solving and adaptability skills
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The work clearly shows how a range of ATL skills were applied with specific evidence of impact on the productâs quality. To deepen this analysis, the student should reflect on obstacles encountered and how overcoming them strengthened their problem-solving and adaptability.

strengths: Compelling narrative linking personal experience to the learning goal
Clear, feasible vision for the guidebook that integrates personal experience with evidence-based research
weaknesses: Success criteria are too general and lack measurable indicators
Action plan does not specify resources, dependencies or clear sequencing
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning section demonstrates strong engagement through a personal narrative and a focused product goal, anchoring the projectâs purpose. However, without detailed, measurable success criteria and a fully specified plan of resources and task sequencing, it will be difficult to assess progress and ensure the planâs feasibility.

strengths: Insightful reflection on personal growth in ATL skills and increased motivation
Specific examples of how the project influenced self-worth and passion discovery
weaknesses: Evaluation of product organization and peer feedback is not directly mapped to the original success criteria
Suggestions for improving content organization lack precise actions tied to criteria
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Reflections effectively capture personal learning gains and motivational developments. To complete the reflective cycle, the student should link each evaluation point and suggested revision back to the defined success criteria and offer concrete steps for reorganizing and refining the product.

annotations.comment: Your explanation of applying creative, critical thinking, and organizational skills during the Canva design process, supported by specific examples, shows strong skill integration impacting product quality.
You provide a compelling narrative linking personal experience to your learning goal; the clear connection to your interest in skincare demonstrates depth of inspiration and purpose.

You effectively describe applying time-management, information-literacy, and communication skills with concrete examples (e.g., scheduling weekly research and reflecting on visual evidence) demonstrating their impact.
When reflecting on content organization, you identify less organized pages; specify how reformatting or content reduction would satisfy success criteria more precisely and enhance readability.
Consider adding specific, measurable success criteria for your learning goal to clarify how you will assess your personal growth in mastering skincare knowledge and Canva design.
Your success criteria outline general expectations but would benefit from detailed, measurable indicators (e.g., case studies included, milestone deliverables) to guide development and evaluation.
The action plan assigns dates and activities effectively but could specify required resources, dependencies, and sequencing to ensure feasibility and clear task progression.
Your product goal articulates a clear, feasible vision for the guidebook, integrating personal experience with evidence-based research objectives, which anchors your project focus.
Reflect more on how specific challenges during Canva use enhanced your problem-solving and adaptability skills, illustrating deeper growth and refinement of these competencies.
Your reflection articulates personal growth in ATL skills and passion discovery, supported by specific reflections on motivational challenges and self-worth recognition.

You evaluate product performance using survey averages and peer feedback, but consider linking each evaluation more directly to your original success criteria for clearer alignment and actionable insights.
